title: Tom Oï¿½Roughley
content:
ï¿½Though logic choppers rule the town,
And every man and maid and boy
Has marked a distant object down,
An aimless joy is a pure joy,ï¿½
Or so did Tom Oï¿½Roughley say
That saw the surges running by,
ï¿½And wisdom is a butterfly
And not a gloomy bird of prey.

ï¿½If little planned is little sinned
But little need the grave distress.
Whatï¿½s dying but a second wind?
How but in zigzag wantonness
Could trumpeter Michael be so brave?ï¿½
Or something of that sort he said,
ï¿½And if my dearest friend were dead
Iï¿½d dance a measure on his grave.ï¿½
meaning:
William Butler Yeats's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Tom Oï¿½Roughley"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
